response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior full-stack developer with strong fundamentals, TDD discipline, and exposure to GIS/geospatial work via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role requires Modern C++ (or Rust) as the primary skill, and the resume shows only limited, dated C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, 2014-2015, plus a bachelor thesis). Primary languages are TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with no Rust experience listed. Geospatial background and willingness to learn are pluses, but the core language gap is significant for a routing tiling pipeline role.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS experience (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk)
Strong low-latency systems background at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services)
Solid engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testable design
concerns: Minimal recent Modern C++ experience; last C/C++ role was ~2015 at National Instruments
No Rust experience listed as an alternative qualification
Recent work is primarily TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the pipeline systems languages required
